label: Ohio
evidence: 68 artworks reference Cuyahoga Falls/Middletown/Cleveland Ohio; co-tagged Pittsburgh PA, Philadelphia, Buffalo NY, Chicago, California; Civil War 1865-67 context. Rijks broader_id chain: Ohio -> United States of America. Rijks dump's TGN equivalent (1034723) contradicts Rijks's own broader_id and artwork descriptions.

label: Montmorency
evidence: Single artwork RP-F-2001-7-699-1 by William Notman titled 'Natural Steps Montmorenci near Quebec'; co-tagged Quebec (Canada) as production_place + spatial. Rijks broader_id chain reaches Canada. Rijks dump's TGN equivalent (7009368) is the French commune, contradicting both.

label: Bournemouth
evidence: 40 artworks including 'strand en pier bij Bournemouth, Engeland'; James Valentine (Scottish photographer); Branksome Tower (suburb of Poole, adjacent to Bournemouth UK). Rijks broader chain: Bournemouth -> England -> Great Britain -> UK. Rijks dump's TGN equivalent (7010737) contradicts both.

label: Place de la Concorde
evidence: Curator-supplied centroid for an areal entity. TGN 7544568 correctly classifies Place de la Concorde as a square (no point centroid in TGN); apply_tgn_areal_flag.py earlier set is_areal=1. For downstream rendering a single-point coord is acceptable. Coord copied from Wikidata Q189503's P625 = (48.8656331 2.3212357). This override resets is_areal=0 and locks coord_method='manual'.

label: Cape of Good Hope
evidence: Curator-supplied centroid for an areal entity. Existing DB coord (-31.0 23.0) was wildly off (interior of South Africa); TGN 7000877 classifies the cape as areal with no centroid. Coord set to the actual cape (-34.3568 18.4734) at the southern tip of the Cape Peninsula. This override resets is_areal=0 and locks coord_method='manual'.

label: Ariano Irpino
evidence: Existing DB coord (42.89 -2.99) lands in Basque Country Spain — wildly wrong. The DB label 'Arriano' is a typo / archaic spelling for Ariano Irpino, an Italian town in Avellino province Campania (NOT Lazio as DB's broader_id chain says — that's a separate hierarchy bug). Identification confirmed by inscription on RP-T-00-492-44 (Louis Ducros 1778): 'Vue de la Ville d'Arriano (Equotuticum)' — Equotuticum is the ancient Roman name associated with this area. Reference image: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ariano_Irpino#/media/File:Veduta_della_città_di_Ariano.jpg shows the same scene Ducros depicted. Coord (41.15277778 15.08888889) per Wikipedia / Wikidata Q55000.

label: Lago d'Agnano
evidence: Existing DB coord (-54.67 -68.0) lands in Tierra del Fuego Patagonia — wildly wrong. Identification confirmed by attached artwork RP-F-F18082 'Gebouw aan het meer van Agnano nabij Napels Italië' (Building at Lake Agnano near Naples Italy) by Giorgio Sommer (1863-1914). Lago d'Agnano is the volcanic crater lake in the Phlegraean Fields near Naples (drained in 1870). Coord (40.82974 14.17045) per Wikidata Q3413605.

label: Cana
evidence: Existing DB coord (42.80 11.42) lands in Tuscany Italy — wildly wrong. Multiple attached artworks explicitly identify biblical Cana in Galilee Israel: RP-F-F13040 'Gezicht op een water bij Cana Israël', RP-F-F13041 'Gezicht op Cana Israël', RP-F-2001-7-1584-9 'Gezicht op Cana te Galilee' by Horatio Herbert Kitchener (who led the 1874-77 Survey of Western Palestine), RP-F-2001-7-1659-88 by Félix Bonfils (the famous Holy Land photographer). Coord (32.75 35.35) per Wikidata Q2633158 (Kafr Kanna — the modern Arab village traditionally identified as biblical Cana).
